matches.rationale: Both are Bash rules triggered by an Xcode build and require running `make project` first.
Both block directly editing generated `.xcodeproj` files.
The rule targets screen ViewModel files and cites the mandatory documented structure. Edit and Write are equivalent here because this constrains file content and structure.
The extracted rule targets ViewController files and requires BaseViewController inheritance and the Common import. Edit and Write are equivalent for this content constraint.
Both constrain UI creation to code rather than storyboard/XIB. The extraction covers the code-only clause but its trigger does not enforce the compound SnapKit clause.
Both target left/right tokens in Swift horizontal constraints and require leading/trailing under SnapKit.
Both require DesignSystem resources for UI styling. The extraction detects direct UIColor and UIFont usage but omits icons, buttons, and other resource forms.
Both prohibit `.then` in newly written Swift content. Edit and Write are equivalent because this is a constraint on file content.
Both require API definitions to use an enum extended with RequestType. Edit and Write are equivalent for this structural content rule.
Both require repositories to be separated into Protocol and Impl types. Edit and Write are equivalent for this structural content rule.
Both target naming of API, Repository, and model/request/response declarations and require server-defined names.
Both require newly created collection-view cells to inherit from BaseCollectionViewCell.
Both block adding static registerId or reuseIdentifier properties inside cell files.
Both prohibit SwiftUI in UI code and require UIKit. Edit and Write are equivalent because this constrains the content of Swift files.

misses.note: No Bash rule enforces the Debug scheme. The compiler self-reported: `Debug/Release 빌드용 스킴 이름 안내 — 참고 정보일 뿐 위반 가능한 규칙 아님`.
No Bash rule enforces the Release scheme. The compiler self-reported: `Debug/Release 빌드용 스킴 이름 안내 — 참고 정보일 뿐 위반 가능한 규칙 아님`.
The only SwiftLint extraction is an Edit rule on Swift files, while the gold rule is a Bash rule about choosing and running post-change validation; the required tool is not the same.
Initializer injection is an explicit sibling bullet in the ViewController/MVVM section, but no distinct extracted rule targets wiring a ViewModel into a ViewController.
Separating route handling into an extension is an explicit sibling bullet in the ViewController/MVVM section, but no distinct extracted rule targets route handling.

unmatched_extracted.note: This is a real Edit-bindable reminder on changed Swift files, but the frozen gold set lists the SwiftLint requirement only as a Bash rule, so strict tool matching leaves this extraction unused.
The document explicitly requires feat/fix/docs-style commit-message convention, and this is a real Bash-bindable rule absent from the frozen gold ruleset.
